title: Request for Proposal (RFP): Project "SmartShelf"
description: Implementation of an AI-Powered Inventory Management & Forecasting System
rfp_content:
# Request for Proposal (RFP): Project "SmartShelf"
**RFP Reference:** SMARTSHELF-RFP-2026-001 **Issue Date:** 2026-04-18 **Deadline:** 2026-05-18

## 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW & BACKGROUND
LiverX is seeking proposals from qualified vendors for the implementation of an AI-Powered Inventory Management & Forecasting System, internally referred to as Project "SmartShelf". The objective of this project is to enhance operational efficiency, reduce inventory holding costs, minimize stockouts, and improve overall supply chain responsiveness through the adoption of advanced AI and data analytics capabilities. The selected solution should provide real-time inventory visibility, accurate demand forecasting, automated reordering recommendations, and seamless integration with existing LiverX systems.

## 2. SCOPE OF WORK
This Request for Proposal (RFP) outlines the requirements for the design, development, implementation, and ongoing support of an AI-powered inventory management and forecasting system.

### 2.1 Deliverables
The selected vendor will be responsible for, but not limited to, the following key deliverables:
- **System Design & Architecture:** Comprehensive documentation of the system's architecture, data models, and technical specifications.
- **AI Model Development:** Development and training of AI/ML models for demand forecasting, anomaly detection, and inventory optimization.
- **System Development & Configuration:** Customization and configuration of the chosen platform to meet LiverX's specific business processes and requirements.
- **Integration:** Seamless integration with existing LiverX enterprise resource planning (ERP), warehouse management systems (WMS), and point-of-sale (POS) systems.
- **Data Migration:** Migration of relevant historical inventory and sales data into the new system.
- **Testing:** Comprehensive testing, including unit, integration, system, and user acceptance testing (UAT).
- **Deployment:** Go-live planning and execution, including cutover strategy and post-deployment support.
- **Training:** Provision of comprehensive training materials and sessions for LiverX administrators and end-users.
- **Documentation:** Provision of detailed system documentation, user manuals, and technical guides.
- **Post-Implementation Support:** Ongoing maintenance, technical support, and performance monitoring as per agreed Service Level Agreements (SLAs).

### 2.2 Out-of-Scope
The following items are considered out-of-scope for this RFP:
- Procurement of new hardware infrastructure beyond what is required for the direct deployment of the AI-powered system (e.g., dedicated servers, advanced networking equipment, if not directly part of the solution).
- Major infrastructure upgrades not directly related to the "SmartShelf" system's functionality.
- Data cleansing of legacy historical data that is not critical for the initial setup and training of the new system.
- Development of entirely new ERP or WMS functionalities.

### 2.3 Vendor Qualifications
Vendors must demonstrate the following qualifications:
- Proven experience in implementing AI-powered inventory management and forecasting solutions for clients in the retail or logistics sector.
- Strong technical expertise in AI/ML, data analytics, cloud platforms, and enterprise system integration.
- Certified professionals in relevant technologies and project management methodologies.
- Financial stability and capacity to deliver a project of this scale.
- Adherence to all LiverX legal, compliance, and information security requirements as outlined in this RFP.
- Ability to provide local support within Jordan or the broader MEA region.
- Possession of all mandatory certifications listed in Section 6.

## 3. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS
Vendors are required to submit a comprehensive proposal addressing all aspects of this RFP. The submission must include, but is not limited to, the following documents:
- **Technical Proposal:** Detailing the proposed solution, methodology, project plan, team structure, and approach to meeting the scope of work.
- **Pricing Breakdown:** A detailed financial proposal as per the format in Section 7.2.
- **Company Profile:** Overview of the vendor's experience, capabilities, and relevant case studies.
- **Resumes of Key Personnel:** CVs of individuals proposed to work on the project, highlighting their relevant experience and certifications.
- **ISO 9001 (Quality Management) Certification:** Must be current.
- **ISO 27001 (Information Security) Certification:** Must be current.
- **CITC (Communications and Information Technology Commission) Operating Licence:** Must be current for operating in Jordan.
- **Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) Compliance Documentation:** Demonstrating adherence to local data protection regulations.
- **NITAQAT Score / Local Content Disclosure:** Percentage of local employees and locally sourced goods/services.
- **Conflict of Interest Declaration:** A signed and notarised declaration (if contract exceeds USD 500,000 equivalent in JOD).
- **Sanctions Self-Declaration Form:** Confirming no affiliations with sanctioned entities.
- **Signed Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA):** LiverX's standard NDA.
- **Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ):** Completed as per Section 6.3.1.
- **ESG Disclosure:** Information regarding carbon footprint, Supplier Code of Conduct, and confirmation of no child/forced labour.
- **Proof of Insurance:** Current certificates of insurance for General Liability, Professional Indemnity, Cyber Liability, and Workers' Compensation as specified in Section 5.2.

## 4. EVALUATION CRITERIA
Proposals will be evaluated based on the following criteria:

| Category                   | Weight | Type         | Description                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|----------------------------|--------|--------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| **Technical Merit**        | 40%    | Scored       | Comprehensive understanding of requirements, innovative solution design, technical architecture, and scalability.                                                                                           |
| **Vendor Experience**      | 20%    | Scored       | Demonstrated experience with similar projects, qualifications of key personnel, and successful project delivery track record.                                                                             |
| **Commercial Proposal**    | 20%    | Scored       | Competitiveness of pricing, adherence to budget, and transparent cost breakdown.                                                                                                                          |
| **Compliance & Risk**      | 10%    | Go/No-Go     | Adherence to all legal, regulatory, security, and compliance requirements outlined in this RFP.                                                                                                            |
| **ESG & Local Content**    | 10%    | Scored       | Alignment with LiverX's ESG values, commitment to sustainability, and contribution to local content/nationalisation initiatives.                                                                          |

## 5. TERMS & CONDITIONS
All contracts arising from this RFP will be subject to LiverX's standard terms and conditions.

5.1 **Conflict of Interest:** Every vendor must submit a signed Conflict of Interest Declaration as part of their bid package. No bid will be evaluated without this document. Vendors must disclose any current, pending, or historical business relationship with LiverX employees, board members, or their immediate family members. Declarations must be signed by an authorised officer of the vendor (VP level or above) and notarised for contracts exceeding USD 500,000 (equivalent to approximately 355,000 JOD based on current rates, to be confirmed at time of signing).

5.2 **Minimum Insurance Requirements:** Vendors must maintain the following insurance policies throughout the engagement and provide current certificates of insurance before contract execution:
    - General Liability (Commercial): USD 2,000,000 per occurrence / USD 4,000,000 aggregate
    - Professional Indemnity (E&O): USD 5,000,000 per claim
    - Cyber Liability: USD 1,000,000 per occurrence
    - Workers' Compensation: As required by applicable law (minimum statutory)
    LiverX must be named as an Additional Insured on all General Liability and Cyber Liability policies. Vendor must provide 30 days written notice of cancellation or material change to any required policy. Insurance must be placed with carriers rated A- or better by AM Best.

5.3 **Governing Law & Jurisdiction:** All contracts arising from LiverX RFPs shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Amman, Jordan, unless resolved through arbitration per Section 5.4.

5.4 **Dispute Resolution:** Parties must attempt in good faith to resolve disputes within 30 calendar days of written notice. If unresolved, disputes proceed to formal mediation administered by the Amman Chamber of Commerce Mediation Centre. If mediation fails within 60 days, disputes are referred to binding arbitration under the Amman Arbitration Centre (AAC) Rules. The arbitration shall be conducted in Arabic and English, with a panel of three arbitrators. The seat of arbitration is Amman, Jordan.

5.5 **NDA & Confidentiality:** Vendors must sign LiverX's standard Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) before receiving any technical specifications, budget information, or evaluation criteria. The NDA period is 5 years from the date of signing. Vendors may not disclose their participation in any LiverX procurement process without prior written consent from the LiverX Chief Procurement Officer. All work product, deliverables, and data accessed during the engagement are work-for-hire and become the sole property of LiverX upon creation. The vendor warrants that all deliverables provided will not infringe on any third-party intellectual property rights.

5.6 **Data Protection & Privacy:** Vendors handling personal data on behalf of LiverX must comply with the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) and its implementing regulations. A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) must be executed before any personal data is transferred to the vendor or its sub-processors. In the event of a data breach, the vendor must notify LiverX within 72 hours of becoming aware of the incident, with a full incident report within 5 business days. Vendor must maintain data residency within the Middle East & Africa (MEA) region unless explicitly approved in writing by LiverX's Data Protection Officer.

5.7 **Subcontractor Disclosure:** Vendors must disclose all subcontractors who will perform any portion of the contracted work at the time of bid submission. No new subcontractors may be engaged after contract award without written approval from LiverX Procurement. The prime vendor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of all subcontractors as if performed by the prime vendor directly. Subcontractors must meet the same insurance and compliance standards as the prime vendor.

5.8 **Anti-Bribery & Anti-Corruption:** Vendors must comply with the Jordanian Anti-Corruption Commission Law (Law No. 62 of 2006 as amended) and, where applicable, the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and the UK Bribery Act 2010. Any vendor found to have offered, paid, or facilitated any bribe or improper inducement will be immediately disqualified and reported to relevant authorities.

5.9 **Termination:** LiverX may terminate the contract for convenience with 30 days written notice. LiverX may terminate the contract for cause if the vendor fails to remedy a material breach within 15 days of written notice.

## 6. COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
Vendors must adhere to the following compliance requirements, which are critical for engagement with LiverX.

6.1 **Mandatory Vendor Certifications:** All vendors must hold and provide evidence of the following certifications at the time of bid submission. Bids without valid certificates will be disqualified.

| Certification                        | Scope                                  | Valid Until         |
|--------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|---------------------|
| ISO 9001 (Quality Management)        | Service delivery & project management  | Must be current     |
| ISO 27001 (Information Security)     | All engagements involving data access  | Must be current     |
| CITC (Operating Licence)             | Operating in Jordan                    | Must be current     |

Additional certifications that strengthen a bid (not mandatory):
- ISO 14001 (Environmental Management)
- SOC 2 Type II
- CMMI Level 3
- Jordan-specific AI / data governance certification

6.2 **Data Governance & Privacy Compliance:** Vendors must comply with all applicable data protection regulations for the jurisdiction in which services are delivered, specifically the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL). Vendors must describe their data residency approach. Data must remain within the Middle East & Africa (MEA) region unless explicit written approval is granted. Vendors must have a documented data classification and handling policy and provide a copy upon request. Personal data must be encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256 or equivalent).

6.3 **Information Security Requirements:** Vendors must complete and return a Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ) with their bid. The VSAQ template is available from the Procurement team on request.

Minimum security controls required:

| Control Area              | Requirement                                                                   |
|---------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Access Management         | MFA enforced for all privileged accounts                                      |
| Vulnerability Management  | Critical patches applied within 72 hours                                      |
| Penetration Testing       | Annual pen test by accredited third party; report available on request        |
| Incident Response         | Documented IRP with RTO ≤ 4 hours for critical systems                        |
| Backup & Recovery         | Daily backups; RPO ≤ 4 hours, RTO ≤ 8 hours                                   |

Vendors must notify LiverX of any security incident affecting LiverX data within 4 hours of detection and provide a full incident report within 72 hours.

6.4 **Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) Requirements:** LiverX is committed to sustainable and responsible business practices. Vendors are expected to align with these values and disclose their own ESG position. Vendors should provide, if available: Carbon footprint / GHG emissions data for their operations; A Supplier Code of Conduct or equivalent policy; Confirmation of no child labour or forced labour in their supply chain. ESG performance is a scored criterion in the Technical Qualification dimension.

6.5 **Local Content & Nationalisation Policy:** Vendors must comply with NITAQAT requirements applicable in Jordan. Vendors must disclose the percentage of local employees / locally sourced goods and services as part of their bid. Minimum local content threshold: 25% of total contract value.

6.6 **Anti-Money Laundering & Sanctions Screening:** All vendors will be screened against the following lists prior to contract award: OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) List, UN Consolidated Sanctions List, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, and Jordan local sanctions list. Vendors must provide a completed Sanctions Self-Declaration Form with their bid. Any vendor flagged during screening will be immediately disqualified pending a compliance review. The procurement team will notify the Legal team within 1 hour of any flag.

6.7 **Health, Safety & Environment (HSE):** For this project, which is primarily software implementation and does not involve physical works or on-site presence beyond typical office visits, detailed HSE requirements for site-specific plans or LTIFR thresholds are not strictly applicable. However, vendors are expected to adhere to general workplace safety standards for their personnel.

6.8 **Regulatory Change:** Vendors must monitor applicable regulations throughout the contract period and notify LiverX promptly of any regulatory change that may affect service delivery, data handling, or contractual obligations. Cost implications of mandatory regulatory changes will be assessed via the Change Order process described in the Economic Template.

## 7. FINANCIAL TERMS
LiverX expects competitive and transparent pricing from all vendors.

7.1 **Budget Policy:** The budget ceiling for this project is 250,000 JOD. Bids exceeding the ceiling by more than 10% will be automatically disqualified unless a written waiver is granted. Budget ranges must be validated against current market benchmarks before the RFP is issued. Finance team sign-off is required for any RFP with a total value exceeding 100,000 JOD. Multi-year contracts must include a cost escalation cap of 10% per year, indexed to CPI. Vendors must disclose their escalation assumptions in their bids.

7.2 **Pricing & Fee Structure:** Vendors must submit pricing in the following mandatory format:

| Line Item              | Unit | Quantity | Unit Price | Total |
|------------------------|------|----------|-----------|-------|
| System Design          | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| AI Model Dev. & Train  | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| System Dev. & Config.  | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Integration            | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Data Migration         | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Testing                | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Deployment             | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Training               | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Documentation          | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Post-Impl. Support     | Mth  |   [X]    |           |       |
| Travel & Expenses      | Lump |    1     |           |       |
| Contingency (max 5%)   | %    |          |           |       |
| **Total Contract Value**|     |          |           |       |

All prices must be stated in JOD, exclusive of applicable taxes. Applicable taxes (VAT / GST at current rate) will be paid separately by LiverX. Fixed-price bids are preferred. If time-and-materials pricing is proposed, vendors must provide day rates per role and a capped estimate with a not-to-exceed total. Prices must remain valid for 60 days from the bid submission deadline.

7.3 **Payment Schedule:** Standard payment milestones:

| Milestone                        | % of Contract Value | Trigger                                     |
|----------------------------------|--------------------|---------------------------------------------|
| Contract Signing / Mobilisation  | 10%                | Signed contract received                    |
| Mid-Project Delivery             | 40%                | Acceptance of agreed interim deliverable    |
| Final Delivery                   | 40%                | Written acceptance of all deliverables      |
| Retention Release                | 10%                | Defects liability period ends (e.g., 90 days post final acceptance) |

Payment terms are Net 30 days from receipt of a valid, undisputed invoice. Invoices must reference the purchase order number and itemise deliverables per the agreed fee schedule. Incomplete invoices will be returned and the payment clock restarted. LiverX reserves the right to withhold payment for deliverables that have not received formal written acceptance.

7.4 **Penalty & Incentive Clauses:**
- **Late Delivery Penalty:** 5% of the milestone value per calendar week of delay, up to a maximum of 10% of the total contract value.
- **Quality Failure Penalty:** If a deliverable fails acceptance testing and requires more than 2 revision cycles, a penalty of 1000 JOD per additional cycle applies.
- **Early Completion Bonus (optional):** Vendors completing the project 2 weeks ahead of schedule with full acceptance may be eligible for a bonus of 10% of contract value, at LiverX's sole discretion.

7.5 **Cost-Control Requirements:** Vendors must submit a detailed cost breakdown with their bid, showing assumptions for effort (person-days), rates, and any third-party costs. Any change in scope must be agreed in writing via a formal Change Order before work begins. Costs incurred without an approved Change Order will not be reimbursed. Vendors must flag any anticipated cost overrun as soon as it is identified — no later than when 10% of the budget is consumed. Surprises at invoice time are not acceptable. Travel and expense claims must comply with LiverX's T&E policy: Economy class for flights under 3 hours; business class requires prior approval. Hotel accommodation capped at 100 JOD per night. All expenses require original receipts and must be claimed within 30 days of incurrence.

7.6 **Financial Qualification Thresholds:** To be considered financially qualified, vendors must demonstrate:

| Criterion                         | Minimum Requirement              |
|-----------------------------------|----------------------------------|
| Annual Revenue (latest year)      | At least 500,000 JOD             |
| Net Income / Operating Profit     | Positive in the last fiscal year |
| Cash & Equivalents                | Sufficient for ≥ 18 months runway|
| Credit Rating (if applicable)     | BBB                              |

Vendors that do not meet these thresholds will be flagged for finance review and may be asked to provide a parent company guarantee or performance bond.

7.7 **Currency & Exchange Rate Risk:** All contracts will be denominated in JOD. If a vendor proposes costs in a foreign currency, they must state the exchange rate used and confirm the rate will be locked at contract signing. Exchange rate fluctuation risk beyond 5% over the contract period may be addressed via a forex adjustment clause, subject to finance team approval.

## 8. TIMELINE & APPENDICES
### 8.1 Project Timeline
- **RFP Issue Date:** 2026-04-18
- **Vendor Questions Deadline:** 2026-05-01
- **RFP Submission Deadline:** 2026-05-18
- **Vendor Presentations/Demos (Shortlisted):** 2026-05-25 - 2026-05-30
- **Final Vendor Selection & Notification:** 2026-06-07
- **Contract Negotiations & Award:** 2026-06-14 - 2026-06-28
- **Project Kick-off:** 2026-07-07

### 8.2 Appendices
- Appendix A: Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ)
- Appendix B: Conflict of Interest Declaration Form
- Appendix C: Sanctions Self-Declaration Form
- Appendix D: Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Template

evaluation.recommendation_reason: NeuralLink Logistics offers the most technically superior solution, exceeding the critical 85% forecasting accuracy requirement with a proprietary AI model. Their strong scalability, integration capabilities, and competitive pricing make them the optimal choice for the 'SmartShelf' project. Other vendors either failed key technical criteria or presented significant risks.

vendors.name: Global Enterprise Systems
flags: Potential for not meeting core AI accuracy requirement

title: testing rfp gen
description: testing rfp gen for LIverX rfp
rfp_content:
# testing rfp gen
**RFP Reference:** LIVERX-RFP-2026-04-001 **Issue Date:** 2026-04-18 **Deadline:** 2026-05-14

## 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW & BACKGROUND
This Request for Proposal (RFP) is issued by LiverX for a project titled "testing rfp gen," which is intended for testing RFP generation processes.

## 2. SCOPE OF WORK
### 2.1 Deliverables
To be defined by LiverX.

### 2.2 Out-of-Scope
To be defined by LiverX.

### 2.3 Vendor Qualifications
Vendors must possess the necessary expertise and resources to fulfill the project requirements. Key qualifications include relevant industry certifications (e.g., ISO 9001, ISO 27001), demonstrated financial stability, and a commitment to legal and ethical compliance as outlined in this RFP. Specific local operating licenses, such as a CITC operating licence for operations in Jordan, are also required.

## 3. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS
Bidders are required to submit the following documents by the stated deadline:
*   Technical Proposal detailing the proposed solution and approach.
*   Pricing Breakdown in the format specified in Section 7.
*   Proof of ISO 9001 (Quality Management) certification.
*   Proof of ISO 27001 (Information Security) certification.
*   Proof of CITC operating licence (if operating in Jordan).
*   Documentation demonstrating compliance with the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL).
*   Disclosure of NITAQAT score (if applicable).
*   Signed Conflict of Interest Declaration (Appendix B).
*   Completed Sanctions Self-Declaration Form (Appendix C).
*   Signed Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) (Appendix D).
*   Completed Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ) (Appendix A).
*   ESG disclosure, if available (e.g., carbon footprint, Supplier Code of Conduct).
*   Proof of all required insurance coverages.

## 4. EVALUATION CRITERIA
Bids will be evaluated based on the following criteria:

| Category | Weight | Type | Description |
|:---------|:-------|:-----|:------------|
| Accuracy | 70%    | Technical | The extent to which the proposed solution meets the technical requirements and objectives. |
| Price    | 10%    | Commercial | Overall cost-effectiveness and adherence to budget constraints. |
| Time     | 20%    | Timeline | Feasibility and efficiency of the proposed project schedule and delivery. |

## 5. TERMS & CONDITIONS
### 5.1 Conflict of Interest
Every vendor must submit a signed Conflict of Interest Declaration as part of their bid package. No bid will be evaluated without this document. Vendors must disclose any current, pending, or historical business relationship with LiverX employees, board members, or their immediate family members. Declarations must be signed by an authorised officer of the vendor (VP level or above) and notarised for contracts exceeding USD 500,000.

### 5.2 Minimum Insurance Requirements
Vendors must maintain the following insurance policies throughout the engagement and provide current certificates of insurance before contract execution:

| Coverage Type | Minimum Limit |
|---|---|
| General Liability (Commercial) | USD 2,000,000 per occurrence / USD 4,000,000 aggregate |
| Professional Indemnity (E&O) | USD 5,000,000 per claim |
| Cyber Liability | USD 1,000,000 per occurrence |
| Workers' Compensation | As required by applicable law (minimum statutory) |

LiverX must be named as an Additional Insured on all General Liability and Cyber Liability policies. Vendor must provide 30 days written notice of cancellation or material change to any required policy. Insurance must be placed with carriers rated A- or better by AM Best.

### 5.3 Governing Law & Jurisdiction
All contracts arising from LiverX RFPs shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan. Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Amman, Jordan, unless resolved through arbitration per Section 5.4.

### 5.4 Dispute Resolution
**Step 1 — Negotiation:** Parties must attempt in good faith to resolve disputes within 30 calendar days of written notice.
**Step 2 — Mediation:** If unresolved, disputes proceed to formal mediation administered by the Amman Chamber of Commerce Mediation Centre.
**Step 3 — Arbitration:** If mediation fails within 60 days, disputes are referred to binding arbitration under the Amman Arbitration Centre (AAC) Rules. The arbitration shall be conducted in Arabic and English, with a panel of three arbitrators. The seat of arbitration is Amman, Jordan.

### 5.5 NDA & Confidentiality
Vendors must sign LiverX's standard Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) before receiving any technical specifications, budget information, or evaluation criteria. The NDA period is 5 years from the date of signing. Vendors may not disclose their participation in any LiverX procurement process without prior written consent from the LiverX Chief Procurement Officer. All work product, deliverables, and data accessed during the engagement are work-for-hire and become the sole property of LiverX upon creation. Vendors must provide an IP infringement warranty.

### 5.6 Data Protection & Privacy
Vendors handling personal data on behalf of LiverX must comply with the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) and its implementing regulations. A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) must be executed before any personal data is transferred to the vendor or its sub-processors. In the event of a data breach, the vendor must notify LiverX within 72 hours of becoming aware of the incident, with a full incident report within 5 business days. Vendor must maintain data residency within the Middle East & Africa (MEA) region unless explicitly approved in writing by LiverX's Data Protection Officer.

### 5.7 Subcontractor Disclosure
Vendors must disclose all subcontractors who will perform any portion of the contracted work at the time of bid submission. No new subcontractors may be engaged after contract award without written approval from LiverX Procurement. The prime vendor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of all subcontractors as if performed by the prime vendor directly. Subcontractors must meet the same insurance and compliance standards as the prime vendor.

### 5.8 Anti-Bribery & Anti-Corruption
Vendors must comply with the Jordanian Anti-Corruption Commission Law (Law No. 62 of 2006 as amended) and, where applicable, the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and the UK Bribery Act 2010. Any vendor found to have offered, paid, or facilitated any bribe or improper inducement will be immediately disqualified and reported to relevant authorities.

### 5.9 Termination
LiverX may terminate the contract for convenience with 30 days written notice to the vendor. In case of termination for cause, the vendor will be given 30 days to remedy the breach.

### 5.10 Liability
The total liability of the vendor under the contract shall be capped at the total contract value. The vendor shall indemnify LiverX against any third-party claims arising from the vendor's negligence or willful misconduct. Neither party shall be liable for any consequential, indirect, or incidental damages.

## 6. COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
### 6.1 Mandatory Vendor Certifications
All vendors must hold and provide evidence of the following certifications at the time of bid submission. Bids without valid certificates will be disqualified.

| Certification | Scope | Valid Until |
|---|---|---|
| ISO 9001 (Quality Management) | Service delivery & project management | Must be current |
| ISO 27001 (Information Security) | All engagements involving data access | Must be current |
| CITC Operating Licence | Operating in Jordan | Must be current |

### 6.2 Data Governance & Privacy Compliance
Vendors must comply with the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL). Vendors must describe their data residency approach. Data must remain within the Middle East & Africa (MEA) region unless explicit written approval is granted. Vendors must have a documented data classification and handling policy and provide a copy upon request. Personal data must be encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256 or equivalent).

### 6.3 Information Security Requirements
Vendors must complete and return a Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ) with their bid. Minimum security controls required:
*   **Access Management**: MFA enforced for all privileged accounts.
*   **Vulnerability Management**: Critical patches applied within 72 hours.
*   **Penetration Testing**: Annual pen test by accredited third party; report available on request.
*   **Incident Response**: Documented IRP with RTO ≤ 4 hours for critical systems.
*   **Backup & Recovery**: Daily backups; RPO ≤ 4 hours, RTO ≤ 4 hours.

Vendors must notify LiverX of any security incident affecting LiverX data within 4 hours of detection and provide a full incident report within 72 hours.

### 6.4 Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) Requirements
LiverX is committed to sustainable and ethical practices. Vendors are expected to align with these values and disclose their own ESG position. Vendors should provide, if available: carbon footprint / GHG emissions data for their operations, a Supplier Code of Conduct or equivalent policy, and confirmation of no child labour or forced labour in their supply chain.

### 6.5 Local Content & Nationalisation Policy
Vendors must comply with NITAQAT requirements applicable in Jordan. Vendors must disclose the percentage of local employees / locally sourced goods and services as part of their bid. Minimum local content threshold: 20% of total contract value.

### 6.6 Anti-Money Laundering & Sanctions Screening
All vendors will be screened against the following lists prior to contract award: OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) List, UN Consolidated Sanctions List, EU Consolidated Sanctions List, and local sanctions lists as applicable in Jordan. Vendors must provide a completed Sanctions Self-Declaration Form with their bid. Any vendor flagged during screening will be immediately disqualified pending a compliance review. The procurement team will notify the Legal team within 1 hour of any flag.

### 6.7 Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) (if applicable)
For engagements involving physical works, on-site presence, or hazardous materials: Vendors must hold a valid HSE Management System certification. A site-specific HSE plan must be submitted before work commences. LTIFR (Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate) must be below 2 per million hours worked. All vendor personnel on LiverX premises must complete a site safety induction before starting work.

### 6.8 Regulatory Change
Vendors must monitor applicable regulations throughout the contract period and notify LiverX promptly of any regulatory change that may affect service delivery, data handling, or contractual obligations. Cost implications of mandatory regulatory changes will be assessed via the Change Order process described in the Economic Template.

## 7. FINANCIAL TERMS
### 7.1 Budget Policy
This RFP has a budget ceiling of 10,000 JOD. Bids exceeding the ceiling by more than 10% will be automatically disqualified unless a written waiver is granted. Finance team sign-off is not required for this RFP. Multi-year contracts must include a cost escalation cap of 10% per year, indexed to CPI. Vendors must disclose their escalation assumptions in their bids.

### 7.2 Pricing & Fee Structure
Vendors must submit pricing in the following mandatory format:

| Line Item | Unit | Quantity | Unit Price | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Deliverable / Service] | | | | |
| Travel & Expenses | Lump | 1 | | |
| Contingency (max 5%) | % | | | |
| **Total Contract Value** | | | | |

All prices must be stated in JOD, exclusive of applicable taxes. Applicable taxes (VAT / GST at current rate) will be paid separately by LiverX. Fixed-price bids are preferred. If time-and-materials pricing is proposed, vendors must provide day rates per role and a capped estimate with a not-to-exceed total. Prices must remain valid for 60 days from the bid submission deadline.

### 7.3 Payment Schedule
Standard payment milestones:

| Milestone | % of Contract Value | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| Contract Signing / Mobilisation | 10% | Signed contract received |
| Mid-Project Delivery | 40% | Acceptance of agreed interim deliverable |
| Final Delivery | 40% | Written acceptance of all deliverables |
| Retention Release | 10% | Defects liability period ends |

Payment terms are Net 30 days from receipt of a valid, undisputed invoice. Invoices must reference the purchase order number and itemise deliverables per the agreed fee schedule. Incomplete invoices will be returned and the payment clock restarted. LiverX reserves the right to withhold payment for deliverables that have not received formal written acceptance.

### 7.4 Penalty & Incentive Clauses
**Late Delivery Penalty:** 5% of the milestone value per calendar week of delay, up to a maximum of 10% of the total contract value.
**Quality Failure Penalty:** If a deliverable fails acceptance testing and requires more than 2 revision cycles, a penalty of 1000 JOD per additional cycle applies.

### 7.5 Cost-Control Requirements
Vendors must submit a detailed cost breakdown with their bid, showing assumptions for effort (person-days), rates, and any third-party costs. Any change in scope must be agreed in writing via a formal Change Order before work begins. Costs incurred without an approved Change Order will not be reimbursed. Vendors must flag any anticipated cost overrun as soon as it is identified — no later than when 10% of the budget is consumed. Surprises at invoice time are not acceptable. Travel and expense claims must comply with LiverX's T&E policy: economy class for flights under 3 hours; business class requires prior approval. Hotel accommodation capped at 100 JOD per night. All expenses require original receipts and must be claimed within 30 days of incurrence.

### 7.6 Financial Qualification Thresholds
To be considered financially qualified, vendors must demonstrate:

| Criterion | Minimum Requirement |
|---|---|
| Annual Revenue (latest year) | At least 1× total contract value |
| Net Income / Operating Profit | Positive in the last fiscal year |
| Cash & Equivalents | Sufficient for ≥ 18 months runway |
| Credit Rating (if applicable) | Not required for this project |

Vendors that do not meet these thresholds will be flagged for finance review and may be asked to provide a parent company guarantee or performance bond.

### 7.7 Currency & Exchange Rate Risk
All contracts will be denominated in JOD. If a vendor proposes costs in a foreign currency, they must state the exchange rate used and confirm the rate will be locked at contract signing. Exchange rate fluctuation risk beyond 5% over the contract period may be addressed via a forex adjustment clause, subject to finance team approval.

## 8. TIMELINE & APPENDICES
### 8.1 Project Timeline
*   **RFP Issue Date:** 2026-04-18
*   **Proposal Submission Deadline:** 2026-05-14
*   **Vendor Selection & Notification:** To be confirmed
*   **Contract Award:** To be confirmed
*   **Project Start Date:** To be confirmed

### 8.2 Appendices
*   **Appendix A:** Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ)
*   **Appendix B:** Conflict of Interest Declaration Form
*   **Appendix C:** Sanctions Self-Declaration Form
*   **Appendix D:** Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Template

rfp_content:
# Request for Proposal (RFP): Project "SmartShelf"
**RFP Reference:** LIVERX-SS-2026-001 **Issue Date:** 2026-04-18 **Deadline:** 2026-05-30

## 1. PROJECT OVERVIEW & BACKGROUND
LiverX is seeking proposals from qualified software development firms for the implementation of an AI-Powered Inventory Management & Forecasting System, Project "SmartShelf". The objective is to replace manual tracking with an automated, predictive system that reduces overstock by 20% and eliminates "stock-outs" for high-demand items. This system is crucial for enhancing our operational efficiency and optimizing our supply chain.

## 2. SCOPE OF WORK
This section details the specific work to be undertaken by the selected vendor, including deliverables, items out-of-scope, and required vendor qualifications.

### 2.1 Deliverables
The successful vendor will be responsible for the following key deliverables:
*   Integration with existing Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software.
*   Development and implementation of a predictive analytics dashboard for procurement officers.
*   Automated "Smart Reordering" triggers based on lead times and seasonal trends.
*   A system that allows QR/RFID scanning via mobile devices without proprietary hardware.
*   A machine learning engine that uses historical sales data to predict future demand with at least 85% accuracy.
*   A cloud-native solution hosted on either AWS or Google Cloud.
*   A system capable of operating offline for up to 4 hours in case of warehouse internet outages, syncing data once reconnected.

### 2.2 Out-of-Scope
The following items are considered out-of-scope for this RFP:
*   Procurement or provision of new mobile scanning hardware.
*   Development of entirely new ERP modules.
*   Physical inventory taking services.

### 2.3 Vendor Qualifications
Vendors must meet the following qualifications:
*   Be a software development firm specializing in LogTech or Supply Chain AI.
*   Demonstrate successful integration of inventory systems for at least two retail or manufacturing clients with over 10,000 SKUs.
*   Hold current ISO 9001 (Quality Management) and SOC 2 Type II (Data Security) certifications.
*   Preferably be a mid-sized firm (30–100 employees) to ensure technical depth and dedicated support teams.

## 3. SUBMISSION REQUIREMENTS
To be considered for this RFP, vendors must submit the following documents via the LiverX Vendor Portal by the deadline of May 30, 2026. The point of contact for this RFP is Sarah Jenkins, Director of Logistics (s.jenkins@smartshelf-logistics.com).
*   **Technical Proposal:** A detailed whitepaper outlining the proposed solution, technical architecture, integration approach, and adherence to all technical requirements.
*   **Pricing Breakdown:** A comprehensive pricing sheet detailing implementation costs, long-term licensing fees, and adherence to the mandatory pricing format specified in Section 7.
*   **ISO 9001 Certification:** Proof of current ISO 9001 (Quality Management) certification.
*   **ISO 27001 Certification:** Proof of current ISO 27001 (Information Security) certification.
*   **SOC 2 Type II Certification:** Proof of current SOC 2 Type II (Data Security) certification.
*   **PDPL Compliance Documentation:** Documentation demonstrating compliance with the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL).
*   **NITAQAT Score (if applicable):** Vendors operating in jurisdictions with nationalization policies should provide their NITAQAT score or equivalent local content percentage.
*   **Conflict of Interest Declaration:** A signed and notarised (for contracts exceeding USD 500,000) Conflict of Interest Declaration.
*   **Sanctions Self-Declaration Form:** A completed Sanctions Self-Declaration Form.
*   **Signed NDA:** A signed copy of LiverX's standard Non-Disclosure Agreement.
*   **Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ):** A completed VSAQ, available upon request from the Procurement team.
*   **ESG Disclosure:** If available, carbon footprint/GHG emissions data, a Supplier Code of Conduct, or confirmation of no child/forced labor.
*   **Proof of Insurance:** Current certificates of insurance demonstrating compliance with the minimum insurance requirements outlined in Section 5.

## 4. EVALUATION CRITERIA
Proposals will be evaluated based on the following criteria, with an overall weighting of 80% Technical and 20% Commercial:

| Category | Weight | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| Algorithmic Accuracy | 38% | Technical | Performance of the vendor’s forecasting engine during a 1-week data trial. |
| Ease of Use | 24% | Technical | UX/UI design of the mobile scanning and dashboard interfaces. |
| Scalability & Support | 18% | Technical | The vendor's ability to support additional warehouse locations globally. |
| Pricing | 20% | Commercial | Implementation costs vs. long-term licensing fees. |

## 5. TERMS & CONDITIONS
### 1. Conflict of Interest
1.1 Every vendor must submit a signed Conflict of Interest Declaration as part of their bid package. No bid will be evaluated without this document.
1.2 Vendors must disclose any current, pending, or historical business relationship with LiverX employees, board members, or their immediate family members.
1.3 Declarations must be signed by an authorised officer of the vendor (VP level or above) and notarised for contracts exceeding USD 500,000.

### 2. Minimum Insurance Requirements
Vendors must maintain the following insurance policies throughout the engagement and provide current certificates of insurance before contract execution:

| Coverage Type | Minimum Limit |
|---|---|
| General Liability (Commercial) | USD 2,000,000 per occurrence / USD 4,000,000 aggregate |
| Professional Indemnity (E&O) | USD 5,000,000 per claim |
| Cyber Liability | USD 1,000,000 per occurrence |
| Workers' Compensation | As required by applicable law (minimum statutory) |

2.1 LiverX must be named as an Additional Insured on all General Liability and Cyber Liability policies.
2.2 Vendor must provide 30 days written notice of cancellation or material change to any required policy.
2.3 Insurance must be placed with carriers rated A- or better by AM Best.

### 3. Governing Law & Jurisdiction
3.1 All contracts arising from LiverX RFPs shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan.
3.2 Any disputes shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of Amman, Jordan, unless resolved through arbitration per Section 4.

### 4. Dispute Resolution
4.1 **Step 1 — Negotiation:** Parties must attempt in good faith to resolve disputes within 30 calendar days of written notice.
4.2 **Step 2 — Mediation:** If unresolved, disputes proceed to formal mediation administered by the Amman Chamber of Commerce Mediation Centre.
4.3 **Step 3 — Arbitration:** If mediation fails within 60 days, disputes are referred to binding arbitration under the Amman Arbitration Centre (AAC) Rules. The arbitration shall be conducted in Arabic and English, with a panel of three arbitrators. The seat of arbitration is Amman, Jordan.

### 5. NDA & Confidentiality
5.1 Vendors must sign LiverX's standard Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) before receiving any technical specifications, budget information, or evaluation criteria.
5.2 The NDA period is 5 years from the date of signing.
5.3 Vendors may not disclose their participation in any LiverX procurement process without prior written consent from the LiverX Chief Procurement Officer.
5.4 All work product, deliverables, and data accessed during the engagement are work-for-hire and become the sole property of LiverX upon creation.

### 6. Data Protection & Privacy
6.1 Vendors handling personal data on behalf of LiverX must comply with the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) and its implementing regulations.
6.2 A Data Processing Agreement (DPA) must be executed before any personal data is transferred to the vendor or its sub-processors.
6.3 In the event of a data breach, the vendor must notify LiverX within 72 hours of becoming aware of the incident, with a full incident report within 5 business days.
6.4 Vendor must maintain data residency within the Middle East & Africa (MEA) region unless explicitly approved in writing by LiverX's Data Protection Officer.

### 7. Subcontractor Disclosure
7.1 Vendors must disclose all subcontractors who will perform any portion of the contracted work at the time of bid submission.
7.2 No new subcontractors may be engaged after contract award without written approval from LiverX Procurement.
7.3 The prime vendor remains fully liable for the acts and omissions of all subcontractors as if performed by the prime vendor directly.
7.4 Subcontractors must meet the same insurance and compliance standards as the prime vendor.

### 8. Anti-Bribery & Anti-Corruption
8.1 Vendors must comply with the Jordanian Anti-Corruption Commission Law (Law No. 62 of 2006 as amended) and, where applicable, the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and the UK Bribery Act 2010.
8.2 Any vendor found to have offered, paid, or facilitated any bribe or improper inducement will be immediately disqualified and reported to relevant authorities.

## 6. COMPLIANCE REQUIREMENTS
### 1. Mandatory Vendor Certifications
All vendors must hold and provide evidence of the following certifications at the time of bid submission. Bids without valid certificates will be disqualified.

| Certification | Scope | Valid Until |
|---|---|---|
| ISO 9001 (Quality Management) | Service delivery & project management | Must be current |
| ISO 27001 (Information Security) | All engagements involving data access | Must be current |
| SOC 2 Type II (Data Security) | All engagements involving data access | Must be current |

### 2. Data Governance & Privacy Compliance
2.1 Vendors must comply with all applicable data protection regulations for the jurisdiction in which services are delivered. For this project, the Jordan Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) is applicable.
2.2 Vendors must describe their data residency approach. Data must remain within the Middle East & Africa (MEA) region unless explicit written approval is granted by LiverX's Data Protection Officer.
2.3 Vendors must have a documented data classification and handling policy and provide a copy upon request.
2.4 Personal data must be encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256 or equivalent).

### 3. Information Security Requirements
3.1 Vendors must complete and return a Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ) with their bid. The VSAQ template is available from the Procurement team on request.
3.2 Minimum security controls required:

| Control Area | Requirement |
|---|---|
| Access Management | MFA enforced for all privileged accounts |
| Vulnerability Management | Critical patches applied within 72 hours |
| Penetration Testing | Annual pen test by accredited third party; report available on request |
| Incident Response | Documented IRP with RTO ≤ 4 hours for critical systems |
| Backup & Recovery | Daily backups; RPO ≤ 1 hour, RTO ≤ 4 hours |

3.3 Vendors must notify LiverX of any security incident affecting LiverX data within 4 hours of detection and provide a full incident report within 72 hours.

### 4. Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) Requirements
4.1 LiverX is committed to sustainable and ethical practices. Vendors are expected to align with these values and disclose their own ESG position.
4.2 Vendors should provide, if available:
- Carbon footprint / GHG emissions data for their operations.
- A Supplier Code of Conduct or equivalent policy.
- Confirmation of no child labour or forced labour in their supply chain.
4.3 ESG performance is a scored criterion in the Technical Qualification dimension.

### 5. Local Content & Nationalisation Policy
5.1 Vendors operating in jurisdictions with nationalization policies (e.g., NITAQAT in Saudi Arabia, In-Country Value in UAE) must comply with applicable requirements in the delivery jurisdiction.
5.2 Vendors must disclose the percentage of local employees / locally sourced goods and services as part of their bid.
5.3 A minimum local content threshold of 20% of the total contract value is preferred.

### 6. Anti-Money Laundering & Sanctions Screening
6.1 All vendors will be screened against the following lists prior to contract award:
- OFAC Specially Designated Nationals (SDN) List
- UN Consolidated Sanctions List
- EU Consolidated Sanctions List
- Jordanian Sanctions List (if applicable)
6.2 Vendors must provide a completed Sanctions Self-Declaration Form with their bid.
6.3 Any vendor flagged during screening will be immediately disqualified pending a compliance review. The procurement team will notify the Legal team within 1 hour of any flag.

### 7. Health, Safety & Environment (HSE) (if applicable)
7.1 For engagements involving physical works, on-site presence, or hazardous materials:
- Vendors must hold a valid HSE Management System certification.
- A site-specific HSE plan must be submitted before work commences.
- LTIFR (Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate) must be below 0.5 per million hours worked.
7.2 All vendor personnel on LiverX premises must complete a site safety induction before starting work.

### 8. Regulatory Change
8.1 Vendors must monitor applicable regulations throughout the contract period and notify LiverX promptly of any regulatory change that may affect service delivery, data handling, or contractual obligations.
8.2 Cost implications of mandatory regulatory changes will be assessed via the Change Order process described in the Economic Template.

## 7. FINANCIAL TERMS
### 1. Budget Policy
1.1 All RFPs must include a stated budget ceiling. For this project, the budget ceiling is USD 325,000. Bids exceeding the ceiling by more than 10% (i.e., USD 357,500) will be automatically disqualified unless a written waiver is granted.
1.2 Budget ranges must be validated against current market benchmarks before the RFP is issued. Finance team sign-off is required for this RFP as its total value exceeds USD 100,000.
1.3 Multi-year contracts must include a cost escalation cap of 10% per year, indexed to the Consumer Price Index (CPI). Vendors must disclose their escalation assumptions in their bids.

### 2. Pricing & Fee Structure
2.1 Vendors must submit pricing in the following mandatory format:

| Line Item | Unit | Quantity | Unit Price | Total |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Deliverable / Service] | | | | |
| Travel & Expenses | Lump | 1 | | |
| Contingency (max 5%) | % | | | |
| **Total Contract Value** | | | | |

2.2 All prices must be stated in USD, exclusive of applicable taxes. Applicable taxes (VAT / GST at current rate) will be paid separately by LiverX.
2.3 Fixed-price bids are preferred. If time-and-materials pricing is proposed, vendors must provide day rates per role and a capped estimate with a not-to-exceed total.
2.4 Prices must remain valid for 60 days from the bid submission deadline.

### 3. Payment Schedule
Standard payment milestones:

| Milestone | % of Contract Value | Trigger |
|---|---|---|
| Contract Signing / Mobilisation | 10% | Signed contract received |
| Mid-Project Delivery | 40% | Acceptance of agreed interim deliverable |
| Final Delivery | 40% | Written acceptance of all deliverables |
| Retention Release | 10% | Defects liability period ends |

3.1 Payment terms are Net 30 days from receipt of a valid, undisputed invoice.
3.2 Invoices must reference the purchase order number and itemise deliverables per the agreed fee schedule. Incomplete invoices will be returned and the payment clock restarted.
3.3 LiverX reserves the right to withhold payment for deliverables that have not received formal written acceptance.

### 4. Penalty & Incentive Clauses
4.1 **Late Delivery Penalty:** 5% of the milestone value per calendar week of delay, up to a maximum of 10% of the total contract value.
4.2 **Quality Failure Penalty:** If a deliverable fails acceptance testing and requires more than 2 revision cycles, a penalty of USD 1,000 per additional cycle applies.
4.3 **Early Completion Bonus (optional):** Vendors completing the project 2 weeks ahead of schedule with full acceptance may be eligible for a bonus of 10% of contract value, at LiverX's sole discretion.

### 5. Cost-Control Requirements
5.1 Vendors must submit a detailed cost breakdown with their bid, showing assumptions for effort (person-days), rates, and any third-party costs.
5.2 Any change in scope must be agreed in writing via a formal Change Order before work begins. Costs incurred without an approved Change Order will not be reimbursed.
5.3 Vendors must flag any anticipated cost overrun as soon as it is identified — no later than when 10% of the budget is consumed. Surprises at invoice time are not acceptable.
5.4 Travel and expense claims must comply with LiverX's T&E policy:
- Economy class for flights under 3 hours; business class requires prior approval.
- Hotel accommodation capped at USD 100 per night.
- All expenses require original receipts and must be claimed within 30 days of incurrence.

### 6. Financial Qualification Thresholds
To be considered financially qualified, vendors must demonstrate:

| Criterion | Minimum Requirement |
|---|---|
| Annual Revenue (latest year) | At least 2× total contract value (USD 650,000) |
| Net Income / Operating Profit | Positive in the last fiscal year |
| Cash & Equivalents | Sufficient for ≥ 18 months runway |
| Credit Rating (if applicable) | BBB |

Vendors that do not meet these thresholds will be flagged for finance review and may be asked to provide a parent company guarantee or performance bond.

### 7. Currency & Exchange Rate Risk
7.1 All contracts will be denominated in USD.
7.2 If a vendor proposes costs in a foreign currency, they must state the exchange rate used and confirm the rate will be locked at contract signing.
7.3 Exchange rate fluctuation risk beyond 5% over the contract period may be addressed via a forex adjustment clause, subject to finance team approval.

## 8. TIMELINE & APPENDICES
### 8.1 Project Timeline
*   **RFP Issue Date:** 2026-04-18
*   **Proposal Submission Deadline:** 2026-05-30
*   **Evaluation Period:** June 2026
*   **Vendor Selection & Contract Award:** July 2026
*   **Pilot Phase Launch:** Within 3 months of contract signing
*   **Full Warehouse Rollout:** Within 6 months of contract signing

### 8.2 Appendices
The following appendices are available upon request or for completion by the vendor:
*   Vendor Security Assessment Questionnaire (VSAQ)
*   Conflict of Interest Declaration Form
*   Sanctions Self-Declaration Form
*   Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA) Template
